title: anacamptis pyramidalis subsp. tanayensis
intro: Anacamptis pyramidalis subsp. tanayensis is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid found in the Philippines. It is characterized by its unique, pyramid-shaped inflorescence and white or pale purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat conditions like fever, sore throat, and digestive issues.
